follow_up_1._comment: Follow Up #1 is handled manually by James. No agent drafts.

follow_up_2.ai_instructions: This is a follow-up to an initial email James sent manually (30+ days ago with no response). Do NOT repeat the initial pitch. Lead with a single sharp data point (valuation gap, performance stat, market context). Keep shorter than the intro email. One clear CTA (call with Paul or send materials). Acknowledge they're busy without being apologetic.

engaged.ai_instructions: This person has shown interest. Be conversational and helpful, not salesy. Reference their specific interest or question from CRM notes if available. The goal is always to get them on a call with Paul. Don't re-pitch the fund basics they already know.

post_meeting.ai_instructions: This person has already met with the team. Be direct and candid. Ask for honest feedback rather than a polite maybe. If CRM notes mention specific concerns or objections, address them proactively. Show confidence in the strategy without being pushy.

due_diligence.ai_instructions: This person is in active due diligence. Be supportive and proactive. Offer to clarify specific items they might be reviewing. Reference Nicole Weirich for data room access. Show awareness that DD is a thorough process. Keep tone patient and detail-oriented.

on_hold.ai_instructions: This person has paused their evaluation. Send a substantive update only. Lead with something concrete: updated performance data, a new valuation comparison, or a market development in power/energy/industrials. Never send a naked check-in. If CRM notes indicate why they paused, acknowledge it briefly. Tone: informative, zero pressure.

declined_cold.ai_instructions: This person previously passed. Be extremely light-touch. Lead with a compelling reason to reconnect: significant performance since they passed, a major market shift in our sectors, or a new fund milestone. Acknowledge that they passed and don't re-pitch from scratch. Tone: respectful, completely no-pressure. Make it easy to say no again.
